documents.t: Introducing Unit Testing for Beginner Devs
Preventing malicious emails from being sent through your domain
Codecon Summit — Embrace the Hacker Way
Winx Day (Luciano dii Souza — O Primo Dev)
If you're a beginner developer (or not), I want to show you that testing can be simple and that complexity only comes according to our needs.
documents.s: Introducing Unit Testing for Beginner Devs

documents.t: Did you know that malicious people could be using your domain to send spoof emails?
documents.s: Preventing malicious emails from being sent through your domain

documents.t:
Between awards, friendships, 12 talks, massive numbers that surpassed 230 MILLION downloads on projects I authored, and several first-time experiences, 2025 took me to such incredible places that I could easily keep things this way forever.
I hope my story inspires you and that you choose to go far beyond your comfort zone 🤘🏻
Conheça um pouco sobre meu impacto.
Lista com a maioria dos projetos que mantenho através da iniciativa open source.
With over 300 million annual downloads across my own projects, I author and maintain critical open-source projects and bring to the stage real-world experiences from systems running at global scale.

🎙️ Talk: Creating a Test Runner: What happens behind the tests?
There's a lot of talk about testing, but what's it like to create a test runner from scratch?
More than that: how do you test a test runner?
documents.s: Codecon Summit — Embrace the Hacker Way

documents.t:
🎙️ Talk: How to Become a Reference in Tech
💡 A talk that brings unique insights and knowledge on how to create impact and inspiration through what we do.

🎙️ Talk: Inventing the New: The Meeting Point Between Human Creativity and AI-Assisted Generation
Improving performance in a legacy system is part of the routine. Inventing algorithms and a brand-new architecture is something completely different.

🎙️ Talk: From Code to the Real World: The Meeting Point between Data, Testing and Security
We'll demystify everything you know about development and explore how to make our application predictable against the unpredictable.

🎓 Mentoring: Open Source and its ecosystem ✨
Open mentoring session on open source, introducing topics and hosting a relaxed Q&A for developers of all levels.

🎙️ Talk: Overcoming the impostor syndrome and the challenges of existing in the industry through open-source.
At a time when many developers feel insecure about the future of the field, bringing up sensitive topics like insecurity, inferiority complex, and self-sabotage, with a light at the end of the tunnel through open source initiatives, was quite challenging but very interesting:

📹 Talk (Live): Meeting Poku.
In a conversation that was both technical and casual, I had the honor of kicking off the very first edition of Open Source Friday by GitHub Brasil, hosted by Pachi Parra.

🎙️ Talk: Creating a High-Performance Multi-Platform Test Runner.
The focus of the talk was on the importance of delegating each problem's responsibilities using native Node.js features.

🎙️ Talk: From Open Source to Microsoft MVP: Interoperability, Security, and Global Impact
In this talk you'll learn from behind the scenes: from supporting MySQL with Bun, Deno, and TypeScript to fixing four critical vulnerabilities in MySQL2, the largest MySQL client in the JavaScript ecosystem.

📹 Palestra (Live): GitHub Beyond Code: overcoming in the job market through open source
Aimed at new developers, I spoke about the impact that open source and GitHub can have on our careers, on the TechTalk program from the MVP Conf channel, hosted by Vanessa Valle and Renata Correa.

🎙️ Talk: Uncovering Tests: Creating Secure and Resilient Systems
Test the functionality of our application? ✅
Break our application before the user or an attacker? That's different.
In addition to the lecture, I had the immense pleasure of recording an episode of the podcast from Curso em Vídeo with, simply, Gustavo Guanabara 🖖

🎙️ Keynote: GitHub Beyond Code: overcoming in the job market through open source
Speaking at a university (USP) is special in itself, but closing the day's event made it even more special ✨
The SSI is an event organized by the university students themselves, and I had the pleasure of being invited to teach through open source, using GitHub as a learning tool.

🎙️ Talk: Uncovering Tests: Creating Secure and Resilient Systems
Test the functionality of our application? ✅
Break our application before the user or an attacker? That's different.

📹 Live (Talk): Overcoming impostor syndrome and the challenges of existing in the market through open source.
An online event organized by Luciano dii Souza, with a relaxed approach and held through the Discord community for developers, aiming to share experiences and knowledge, especially for those who are just starting in the field.
documents.s: Winx Day (Luciano dii Souza — O Primo Dev)

documents.t: About Nice to meet you! 👋🏻 I'm Weslley, but you can call me "Well" and I live in Brazil. I impact millions of developers and projects around the world through the open-source, especially in the JavaScript ecosystem. What do I do? ✨ I'm an end-to-end developer, accumulating over 10 million monthly downloads in my authored open-source projects. 🚀 I actively contribute to numerous high-impact projects that, together, collectively amount to over 550 million downloads monthly. Some of them are: MySQL2 ➕ ⚡️ Fast mysqljs/mysql compatible MySQL driver for Node.js. AWS SSL Profiles ➕ 📜 AWS RDS SSL certificates bundles (created under mysqljs organization) . lru.min ➕ 🔥 An extremely fast and efficient LRU cache for JavaScript. @types/node ➗ 🐢 Node.js JavaScript runtime. named-placeholders ➗ 🐬 PDO-style SQL named placeholders to unnamed compiler. quibble ➗ 🃏 Makes it easy to replace require'd dependencies. CEP Promise ➗ 📪 ZIP code search integrated directly with Correios, ViaCEP, and other services. word-wrap ➗ 🅰 Wrap words to a specified length. ➕ Projects I maintain. ➗ Numbers for these contributions might not be directly mine, but it's fascinating trying to measure the impact my contributions can have directly and indirectly. tip By clicking on the name of each project in the table, you go immediately to my contributions to each of them 👨🏻‍💻 MySQL2 🐬 I'm proud to be a maintainer of MySQL2, the faster and most downloaded MySQL driver for Node.js, Bun, and Deno. It's used by more than 1 million public projects on GitHub. Poku 🐷 I'm also the creator of Poku, a high-performance test runner that simplifies the way you test your JavaScript code. Awesome You 🐜 I'm the founder of Awesome You, an initiative that breaks the popularity barrier and brings a fair environment by recognizing open source maintainers by several metrics of real-world impact. Microsoft Most Valuable Professional (MVP) 🏆 In May 2025, I received the Microsoft MVP award in the "Developer Technologies" category. In Brazil, only 22 people have been awarded in this category through "Web Development" Technology Area until May 2025. It means 0.000407% of brazilian developers. The Microsoft MVP program globally recognizes exceptional technical leaders for their deep knowledge, expertise, and influence. Through voluntary and high-impact contributions, MVPs transform communities and solve real-world problems, especially within the Microsoft technologies ecosystem. Media Kit 🎉 Want to invite me to collaborate at your event or company? Check out my Media Kit here. Contact 🤝 Feel free to contact me and follow me on social media. I'm always open to discussing creative ways to make our lives easier and safer through programming. 🏅 Certificates Credly

Let's Collab 🤘🏻 info Available in Portuguese only 🇧🇷 📌 Weslley Araújo Open Sourcerer, Microsoft MVP, mantenedor do MySQL2 e criador do test runner Poku. Impacta milhões de desenvolvedores e projetos globalmente através do open source. 🗺️ São Paulo, SP — Brasil Sobre Weslley Araújo é um desenvolvedor independente com mais de 10 anos de experiência, mantenedor do MySQL2 — o cliente MySQL mais usado do ecossistema JavaScript — e criador do Poku, um test runner de alta performance que desafia a própria linguagem de programação. Reconhecido como Microsoft MVP, Weslley é especializado em desenvolver para desenvolvedores, criando e mantendo projetos críticos no ecossistema JavaScript, impactando ativamente milhões de projetos e desenvolvedores globalmente através do Open Source. 🇧🇷 Além disso, ele compartilha seu conhecimento através de palestras, painéis e mentorias por todo o país. 📊 Impacto Ao combinar somente projetos open source autorais, são milhões de instalações e downloads recorrentes: Downloads nos últimos 365 dias: Downloads nos últimos 30 dias: 🔄 Última atualização: 📜 Histórico 2021: 91 downloads 2022: 9.470 downloads 2023: 9.733 downloads 2024: 46.912.782 downloads 2025: 233.754.383 downloads 2026: Essa história ainda está sendo contada ✨ 🚀 Interesses de Colaboração Palestras, mentorias e painéis sobre as melhores práticas de desenvolvimento: 🤝 Open source e todo seu ecossistema. 🔬 Como as coisas funcionam por baixo dos panos. 🧪 Testes automatizados e cobertura de testes. ⚡️ Alta performance, escalabilidade, segurança e resiliência. 💡 Uso ético, criativo e realista da Inteligência Artificial. 🔐 Técnicas de compliance e governança de software. 🫶🏻 Sim, eu amaria uma sugestão de tema para o seu evento. 🎙️ Palestras Weslley já palestrou em eventos dos mais diversos públicos e tamanhos, incluindo Codecon Summit, Roga DX, Microsoft Reactor, DevFest (Google Developer Groups), entre outros. ➡️ Palestras realizadas. ➡️ Próximas palestras. Já são mais de 3.000 pessoas impactadas diretamente ao somar eventos presenciais e online. 👾 Vulnerabilidades Corrigidas (CVEs) Vulnerabilidade Severidade Impacto após 30 dias corridos da correção Remote Code Execution (RCE) Critical (9.8) 11.509.199 (11,5 milhões de instalações) Prototype Pollution High (8.2) 11.207.924 (11,2 milhões de instalações) Prototype Poisoning Medium (6.5) 10.543.754 (10,5 milhões de instalações) Use of Web Browser Cache Containing Sensitive Information Medium (6.5) 11.153.226 (11,1 milhões de instalações) 🏆 Microsoft MVP Como Microsoft MVP, Weslley é reconhecido por sua contribuição significativa para a comunidade de desenvolvedores, incluindo palestras, artigos, conteúdos educacionais e, especialmente, através de suas contribuições no ecossistema open source: 🔐 Corrigiu vulnerabilidades críticas em um projeto usado por milhões de desenvolvedores. 🔧 Inclui compatibilidade para TypeScript em diversos projetos open source terceiros. 🎓 Cria artigos e conteúdos técnicos sobre desenvolvimento web e tecnologias emergentes. ✨ Garante suporte para Deno, Bun e Cloudflare Workers para projetos compatíveis apenas com Node.js. 🧪 Criou um test runner que permite usar a mesma suíte de testes com Node.js, Deno, Bun e Cloudflare Workers. 💡 Criou um gerenciador de cachê em memória utilizado por um dos principais protocolos de Inteligência Artificial (MCP). 📦 Principais Projetos 🐷 Poku: O único test runner compatível com todos os runtimes para JavaScript no backend da atualidade (incluindo TypeScript). Traz a sintaxe nativa do JavaScript para os testes, democratizando o acesso a testes automatizados para desenvolvedores de todos os níveis. 🔥 lru.min: Implementação rigorosamente eficiente, altamente e estupidamente performática para cacheamento em memória baseado no algoritmo Least Recently Used para JavaScript. Frequentemente usado em protocolos de Inteligência Artificial como o MCP devido sua alta taxa de compatibilidade. 🐬 MySQL2 (Mantenedor): O cliente MySQL mais performático e baixado para Node.js, Bun, Deno e Cloudflare Workers, utilizado por mais de 1 milhão de projetos públicos no GitHub. ✨ Iniciativas 🐜 Awesome You: Iniciativa que quebra a barreira da popularidade, trazendo um ambiente justo ao reconhecer mantenedores open source por diversas métricas de impacto no mundo real. 🐢 NodeBR & 🇧🇷 JSConf Brasil (Co-organizador e Community Manager): Comunidade que promove o desenvolvimento e a colaboração entre desenvolvedores de Node.js e seu ecossistema no Brasil. 🎧 Participações em Podcasts Programa Canal Host Tema Data Experience Podcast Curso em Vídeo Gustavo Guanabara "Eu achava que não era programador de verdade" 25/02/26 DECODE FIAP FIAP André David Chef de IA, buscas no Reddit e mudanças na Microsoft 15/08/25 Papinho Tech Gustavo Caetano Gustavo Caetano O poder do Open Source | Tema Livre 09/07/25 📆 Eventos Organizados Evento Realização Função Data Redescrobrindo o Open Source Microsoft Reactor, NodeBR e Awesome You Idealizador e Organizador 27/06/25 🖖 Redes Sociais ±4.240 seguidores ±1.5mil estrelas (projetos autorais) e ±4.4mil estrelas (co-mantenedor) ±1.250 seguidores 🔄 Última atualização: 28/03/2026. Fique à vontade para entrar em contato através da rede social de sua preferência. 🏅 Certificados Credly

Projects Here you'll discover a little of the history of each project I maintain. OSI: Open Source Initiative. This site (yes, this one) 👨🏻‍🎨 Have you seen that cool portfolio but have no idea how it was made? My portfolio is open-source and you can not only look at it, but create a fork and adapt it to use in your own portfolio. It also serves as a small showcase of my work in frontend, since most of my projects are focused in backend. wellwelwel/weslley.io License: AGPL-3.0 Node.js JavaScript TypeScript GitHub GitHub Actions Apache Docker Docusaurus MDX React Sass Cloudflare Visual Studio Code Poku 🐷 As a dependency creator, the initial idea was to create a test runner that would truthfully ensure that my projects worked for Node.js, Deno and Bun at the same time, using the same test suite to do so. Then an idea came to me along the way: What if I made testing easy? As result, a zero-configuration test runner has arisen, which goes back to the JavaScript origins and brings back its native syntax essence for tests, plus performing up to 5 times faster than the most popular test runner in the entire JavaScript ecosystem in a simple benchmark. wellwelwel/poku License: MIT Node.js Bun Deno JavaScript TypeScript Bash GitHub GitHub Actions Docker Codecov npm Docusaurus MDX React Sass Algolia Cloudflare Visual Studio Code MySQL2 🐬 I'm proud to be a maintainer of MySQL2, one of the faster MySQL clients in the entire JavaScript ecosystem and the most downloaded for Node.js, Bun, and Deno, as well as being used by more than 1 million public projects on GitHub. It's the main component behind projects such as Sequelize, Drizzle ORM, TypeORM, Knex, among other frameworks (ORM) for MySQL connections. On March 24, 2024, I migrated the entire MySQL2 test suite to Poku, ensuring compatibility between Node.js, Bun and Deno. sidorares/node-mysql2 License: MIT Node.js Bun Deno JavaScript TypeScript MySQL Amazon Web Services (AWS) Azure Azure SQL Database Cloudflare Workers Bash GitHub GitHub Actions Docker Codecov npm Docusaurus MDX React Sass SVPS: Simplifying VPS 🚀 The original idea was just to automate some personal tasks when I purchased a “zeroed” VPS or EC2, but it ended up becoming an extremely robust ORM for Ubuntu servers. As well as repairing and installing the most commonly used tools on servers in a granular approach, SVPS also enables you to create proxies automatically, directing ports to any domain or subdomain you want. This project is currently one of the main sources of my company's profits. wellwelwel/svps License: MIT Bash Node.js JavaScript TypeScript Linux Apache PHP Composer MySQL Amazon Web Services (AWS) Azure Azure DevOps GitHub GitHub Actions Docker npm Visual Studio Code lru.min 🔥 A performance investigation that resulted in one of the most performant and consistent LRU-based cache projects in the entire JavaScript ecosystem. It's currently used by more than 170,000 public repositories on GitHub and is downloaded more than 5 million times a month. wellwelwel/lru.min License: MIT Node.js Bun Deno JavaScript TypeScript GitHub GitHub Actions Codecov npm Visual Studio Code AWS SSL Profiles 📜 Originated from a confusion after AWS changed all its authentication certificates for RDS services, breaking systems that had been working perfectly for years, this project came about to unify the certificates in one place. This way, any maintainer of a project that depends on these certificates (mysql, mysql2 and pg in particular) no longer has to worry about unexpected updates. Despite its simplicity, it's downloaded more than 6 million times a month. mysqljs/aws-ssl-profiles License: MIT Node.js JavaScript TypeScript MySQL Amazon Web Services (AWS) Azure Azure SQL Database GitHub GitHub Actions npm Packages Update 📦 After noticing that all the NPM package updaters had several dependencies, as well as megabytes, this project began as a personal challenge to create the same one, but without any dependencies. Today, the project has evolved and is used especially to update monorepositories dynamically, weighing around 40KB in the final total of its installation. wellwelwel/packages-update License: MIT Node.js JavaScript TypeScript GitHub GitHub Actions npm Visual Studio Code Poku Theme 🎨 A cute "gothic pastel" theme inspired by Poku's test runner visual identity. While Poku test runner makes testing easier, Poku's theme makes it easy to read your code, since it's not just about combining colors, but establishing consistency between different language syntaxes. You can check with your favorite theme if the types in TypeScript and PHP have the same color, because in Poku Theme it will 😏 pokujs/themes License: MIT Node.js JavaScript TypeScript GitHub GitHub Actions Visual Studio Code Local Ubuntu VPS and EC2 ☘️ A completely cost-free option for creating VPS, KVM, EC2, and Azure environments locally, perfect for development and testing. Sounds complex, right? But just download the Docker image and use it with everything ready: docker pull wellwelwel/vps. wellwelwel/local-vps-and-ec2 License: MIT Bash Linux Docker Amazon Web Services (AWS) Azure Azure DevOps GitHub GitHub Actions Visual Studio Code There are more! 🚧 There are A LOT of projects and over time I promise to put each one of them here, but just look at my GitHub and there's everything there.
